forecast-period.text: A cold front moves across the southeast tonight followed by another front on Saturday. A weak high pressure system moves across from the west on Sunday. A significant low pressure system moves over waters to the south of SA on Monday and Tuesday reaching western Bass Strait Tuesday afternoon. A high pressure system follows the low moving across northern parts of SA mid week.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Isolated showers about the agricultural areas, western coasts and south of the Pastoral districts, tending scattered about windward coasts and ranges. Maximum temperatures slightly below average, grading to near average in the north. West to southwesterly winds extending throughout, fresh at times about the coasts. Winds tending southwest to southerly in the west later.

Isolated showers about western coasts and the southern agricultural area, scattered about the southern coasts and the southeast. Possible thunderstorms about southern coasts. Northwesterly winds ahead of a west to southwesterly change moving across western and central districts, reaching eastern districts late.

Isolated light showers about the southern agricultural area, mainly about windward coasts and ranges. Showers developing about the west during the afternoon. Maximum temperatures below average, grading to above average in the northwest. West to southwesterly winds, fresh at times about the central and eastern coasts, tending north to northeasterly across western districts.

Gusty showers, possible thunderstorms and patchy rain broadly south of a line from Coober Pedy to Leigh Creek. Maximum temperatures generally above average. Freshening northerly winds, shifting north to northwesterly ahead of a fresh to strong and gusty westerly change in the west, reaching central districts at night.

text.p: Gusty showers and patchy rain about central and southeastern districts. Isolated showers about the west and south of the pastoral districts. Possible thunderstorms about the southern coasts. Maximum temperatures generally above average. A fresh to strong and gusty westerly change extending throughout. winds easing from the west later.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Tuesday: Less than 5 mm about the Pastoral districts south of about Coober Pedy to Leigh Creek, increasing to 2 to 10 mm about the northern agricultural area, 5 to 15 mm about the southern agricultural area and increasing to 15 to 30 mm about the Mount Lofty Ranges, Kangaroo Island and the South East, with isolated falls in these areas of 30 to 60 mm.
